master.title: Complete XiXteen Critical Thinking Curriculum
master.description: Full 16-skill curriculum with all item feedback and classroom commentary.

synthesis.title: XiXteen Synthesis Paper
synthesis.description: A concise overview of the 16 skills as essential aspects of critical thinking.

skills.name: Clarify the Claim
skills.testableTask: Find the exact claim being made.
skills.essentialQuestion: What exactly is the speaker asking us to accept?

skills.name: Define Key Terms
skills.testableTask: Find the word or phrase that needs a clearer meaning.
skills.essentialQuestion: Which word or phrase must be made clearer before the argument can be fairly judged?

skills.name: Find the Argument
skills.testableTask: Find the conclusion, reason, evidence, example, or side comment.
skills.essentialQuestion: What role does each sentence play in the argument?

skills.name: Spot Hidden Assumptions
skills.testableTask: Find the unstated idea the argument needs.
skills.essentialQuestion: What unstated idea must be true for the reason to support the conclusion?

skills.name: Test Relevance
skills.testableTask: Decide whether a fact actually matters for the claim.
skills.essentialQuestion: Which fact actually helps us judge the claim?

skills.name: Evaluate Evidence Quality
skills.testableTask: Pick the strongest evidence from the details given.
skills.essentialQuestion: How strong is the evidence for this claim?

skills.name: Check Source Reliability
skills.testableTask: Judge a source using expertise, motives, method, and openness.
skills.essentialQuestion: How much trust should we place in this source, and why?

skills.name: Detect Logical Gaps
skills.testableTask: Find the step that does not follow.
skills.essentialQuestion: Does the conclusion really follow from the evidence?

skills.name: Recognize Common Fallacies
skills.testableTask: Match a bad argument to its mistake.
skills.essentialQuestion: What recognizable bad move does this argument make?

skills.name: Think Probabilistically
skills.testableTask: Choose the answer that handles chance and uncertainty well.
skills.essentialQuestion: How should uncertainty affect what we expect?

skills.name: Use Statistical Sense
skills.testableTask: Read numbers with the right context.
skills.essentialQuestion: What does this number mean in context?

skills.name: Separate Correlation and Causation
skills.testableTask: Explain why a cause claim is not proven yet.
skills.essentialQuestion: Has the argument shown that one thing caused another?

skills.name: Consider Alternative Explanations
skills.testableTask: Pick another explanation that also fits the facts.
skills.essentialQuestion: What else could explain the same facts?

skills.name: Recognize Cognitive Biases
skills.testableTask: Name the thinking bias shown in a choice.
skills.essentialQuestion: What mental pull is shaping this judgment?

skills.name: Weigh Tradeoffs and Consequences
skills.testableTask: Find the clearest cost, benefit, risk, or second effect.
skills.essentialQuestion: What does this choice gain, and what does it give up or risk?

skills.name: Update Beliefs Responsibly
skills.testableTask: Change confidence by the right amount.
skills.essentialQuestion: How much should new evidence change confidence?
